topic: Should schools ban or embrace AI tools like ChatGPT for homework?
text: It's very ongoing topic, because now practically every person touches these technologies every single day. Sometimes even don't notice it, that means the AI has come into our lives really smoothly. Not only our life has been changing for last years, but also the way how people get, extract and process information. Especially, it comes to children's education. AI has already changed the way how kids prepare their homework, learn new topics, and get any information they want in just a couple of clicks. I wish I had some of these tools when I was studying at school or preparing to the exam at University. Needless to say, that it could change a lot. However, I can't help saying that in many cases these fast-thinking tools, having any answers for any questions, can disrupt our ability to think, to find information, to put our effort in order to force our neurons thinking. Unfortunately, there is only one way for us to adapt to these changes, as humanity has done this all the time.

correctedText: It's a very popular topic because now practically everyone uses these technologies every single day. Sometimes they even don't notice it, which means that AI has come into our lives really smoothly. Not only has our life been changing over the last few years, but also the way that people get, extract**,** and process information. Especially, it comes to children's education. AI has already changed the way that kids prepare their homework, learn new topics**,** and get information they want in just a couple of clicks. I wish I had known about some of these tools when I was studying at school or preparing for exams at university. Needless to say, this could have changed a lot. However, I can't help saying that in many cases these fast tools, having the answers to any question, can disrupt our ability to think, to find information**,** and to put effort into making our neurons think. Unfortunately, there is only one way for us to adapt to these changes, as humanity has always done.
